Macaglia disperma, also known as Maytenus boaria, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, preventing infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Macaglia disperma include maytansine, maytansol, and maytanbutanol, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Macaglia disperma include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
